certificate.en: an official document that shows you have completed a course or have a particular qualification

ex.en: She received a certificate for completing the course.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 과정을 수료했다는 증명서를 받았다.

certification.en: an official document or process that proves someone has met a required standard

ex.en: He earned a certification in project management.
ex.ko: 그는 프로젝트 관리 자격증을 취득했다.

certification.en: official confirmation that something is true, accurate, or meets a standard

ex.en: The product passed safety certification before release.
ex.ko: 그 제품은 출시 전 안전 인증을 통과했다.

certify.en: officially state that something is true or meets a required standard

ex.en: The lab certified that the water was safe to drink.
ex.ko: 그 연구소는 물이 마시기에 안전하다고 증명했다.

certify.en: officially recognize someone as qualified to do a particular job

ex.en: She is certified to teach yoga.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요가를 가르칠 자격을 공인받았다.

chancellor.en: the head of government in some countries, such as Germany

ex.en: The German chancellor gave a speech on the economy.
ex.ko: 독일 총리가 경제에 관한 연설을 했다.

chancellor.en: the official head of a university, often a mostly honorary position

ex.en: She was appointed chancellor of the university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 대학교의 총장으로 임명되었다.

chaotic.en: in a state of complete confusion and disorder

ex.en: The airport was chaotic after the flights were cancelled.
ex.ko: 항공편이 취소된 후 공항은 혼란스러웠다.

charming.en: very pleasant or attractive in a way that charms people

ex.en: He has a charming smile that puts everyone at ease.
ex.ko: 그는 모두를 편안하게 만드는 매력적인 미소를 가지고 있다.

cheat.en: act dishonestly to gain an advantage, especially in a test, game, or business

ex.en: He was caught cheating on the exam.
ex.ko: 그는 시험에서 부정행위를 하다가 걸렸다.

cheat.en: deceive someone in order to get money or something valuable from them

ex.en: The company cheated investors out of millions of dollars.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 투자자들에게서 수백만 달러를 속여 빼앗았다.

cheat.en: be sexually unfaithful to your husband, wife, or partner

ex.en: She found out that he had been cheating on her.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 바람을 피워왔다는 것을 알게 되었다.

cheat.en: a person who behaves dishonestly to gain an advantage

ex.en: Everyone in the club knew he was a cheat.
ex.ko: 클럽의 모든 사람은 그가 사기꾼이라는 것을 알고 있었다.

checkpoint.en: a place where people or vehicles are stopped and checked, especially at a border

ex.en: Soldiers were stationed at the checkpoint.
ex.ko: 군인들이 검문소에 배치되어 있었다.

checkpoint.cue: 게임 중 진행 상황을 저장하는 지점
checkpoint.en: a point in a game or process where your progress is saved or checked

ex.en: The game saves your progress at each checkpoint.
ex.ko: 그 게임은 체크포인트마다 진행 상황을 저장한다.

cheeky.en: showing a lack of respect in a way that is amusing or slightly annoying

ex.en: The cheeky kid stuck his tongue out at the teacher.
ex.ko: 그 되바라진 아이는 선생님에게 혀를 내밀었다.

cheerfully.en: in a happy and positive way

ex.en: She waved cheerfully at her friends.
ex.ko: 그녀는 친구들에게 쾌활하게 손을 흔들었다.

cheerfulness.en: the quality of being happy and positive

ex.en: Her cheerfulness made everyone around her feel better.
ex.ko: 그녀의 쾌활함은 주변 사람들의 기분을 좋게 만들었다.

chemotherapy.en: the treatment of disease, especially cancer, using powerful chemicals

ex.en: She is undergoing chemotherapy for breast cancer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 유방암 치료를 위해 화학요법을 받고 있다.

cherished.en: loved and cared for deeply, often for a long time

ex.en: This necklace is a cherished gift from my grandmother.
ex.ko: 이 목걸이는 할머니에게서 받은 소중한 선물이다.

cherry.en: a small round red or black fruit with a hard stone in the middle

ex.en: She bought a bag of fresh cherries at the market.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시장에서 신선한 체리 한 봉지를 샀다.

cherry.en: a tree that produces cherries or has beautiful pink or white blossoms

ex.en: The cherry trees bloomed early this spring.
ex.ko: 올봄에는 벚나무가 일찍 꽃을 피웠다.

chew.en: crush food between your teeth before swallowing

ex.en: Remember to chew your food slowly.
ex.ko: 음식을 천천히 씹는 것을 기억하세요.

childish.en: behaving in a way that shows a lack of maturity, typical of a child

ex.en: Stop being so childish and apologize.
ex.ko: 유치하게 굴지 말고 사과해.

chimney.en: a vertical pipe that lets smoke escape from a fire, usually through the roof

ex.en: Smoke rose from the chimney on a cold morning.
ex.ko: 추운 아침 굴뚝에서 연기가 피어올랐다.

chip.en: a small piece broken or cut off from something larger

ex.en: A chip of paint fell off the old fence.
ex.ko: 낡은 울타리에서 페인트 조각이 떨어졌다.

chip.en: a thin, crisp slice of fried potato eaten as a snack

ex.en: He was eating a bag of chips while watching TV.
ex.ko: 그는 TV를 보며 감자칩 한 봉지를 먹고 있었다.

chip.en: a small piece of silicon inside a computer or electronic device that performs electronic functions

ex.en: The new phone has a faster chip inside.
ex.ko: 그 신형 휴대폰에는 더 빠른 칩이 내장되어 있다.

chip.en: break a small piece off the edge of something

ex.en: She chipped a tooth on a hard candy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 딱딱한 사탕을 먹다가 이가 깨졌다.

chop.en: cut something into pieces with a knife or axe

ex.en: She chopped the onions finely.
ex.ko: 그녀는 양파를 잘게 썰었다.

chop.en: a small piece of meat, often including a bone, cut from the ribs

ex.en: We had pork chops for dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁으로 돼지고기 찹을 먹었다.

chunk.en: a large solid piece of something

ex.en: He cut the cheese into thick chunks.
ex.ko: 그는 치즈를 두꺼운 덩어리로 잘랐다.

chunk.en: a large amount or part of something, such as time or money

ex.en: Rent takes a big chunk of my salary.
ex.ko: 월세가 내 월급의 상당한 부분을 차지한다.

circuit.en: a complete path that an electric current can flow through

ex.en: The circuit was broken, so the light didn't turn on.
ex.ko: 회로가 끊겨서 불이 켜지지 않았다.

circuit.cue: 특정 장소를 한 바퀴 도는 길
circuit.en: a route or journey that goes around a particular area and returns to the start

ex.en: The bus follows a circuit around the city center.
ex.ko: 그 버스는 시내 중심가를 순회하는 노선을 따른다.

circuitry.en: the system of electronic circuits inside a device

ex.en: The circuitry inside the laptop was damaged by water.
ex.ko: 노트북 내부 회로가 물에 손상되었다.

circumstance.en: a fact or condition connected with or relevant to an event or situation

ex.en: Under the circumstances, we decided to postpone the trip.
ex.ko: 그런 상황에서 우리는 여행을 미루기로 했다.

citywide.en: happening or existing across an entire city

ex.en: The mayor announced a citywide curfew.
ex.ko: 시장은 시 전역에 통행금지를 발표했다.

clam.en: a shellfish with a hard round shell that lives in sand or mud

ex.en: They dug for clams on the beach.
ex.ko: 그들은 해변에서 조개를 캤다.

clap.en: hit your hands together to show approval or make a sound

ex.en: The audience clapped loudly after the concert.
ex.ko: 관객들은 콘서트가 끝난 후 크게 박수를 쳤다.

clap.en: a sudden loud sound, especially of thunder

ex.en: A clap of thunder woke the baby up.
ex.ko: 천둥소리에 아기가 잠에서 깼다.

clarify.en: make something clearer or easier to understand

ex.en: Could you clarify what you mean by that?
ex.ko: 그게 무슨 뜻인지 명확히 설명해 주시겠어요?

classification.en: the act of putting things into groups according to their type

ex.en: The classification of animals is based on their characteristics.
ex.ko: 동물의 분류는 그 특성에 따라 이루어진다.

classification.en: an official category showing how secret or restricted a document is

ex.en: The report has a top-secret classification.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 1급 기밀 등급을 받았다.

clause.en: a group of words containing a subject and a verb, forming part of a sentence

ex.en: This sentence has two clauses joined by 'and'.
ex.ko: 이 문장은 'and'로 연결된 두 개의 절로 이루어져 있다.

clause.en: a separate section of a legal document, such as a contract

ex.en: Please read the cancellation clause carefully.
ex.ko: 취소 조항을 주의 깊게 읽어 보세요.

clearness.en: the quality of being easy to understand or see clearly

ex.en: He explained the process with great clearness.
ex.ko: 그는 그 과정을 매우 명확하게 설명했다.

clearness.en: the quality of a liquid being clear and not cloudy

ex.en: The clearness of the lake water surprised the tourists.
ex.ko: 그 호수 물의 투명함이 관광객들을 놀라게 했다.

clerical.en: relating to office work such as filing or typing

ex.en: She works in a clerical position at the hospital.
ex.ko: 그녀는 병원에서 사무직으로 일한다.

clerical.en: relating to priests or the clergy

ex.en: He wore a clerical collar to the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의에 성직자용 칼라를 착용하고 갔다.

client.en: a person who pays for services or advice from a professional or business

ex.en: The lawyer met with her client to discuss the case.
ex.ko: 변호사는 사건을 논의하기 위해 의뢰인을 만났다.

client.en: a computer or program that requests services from a server over a network

ex.en: The client sends a request to the server.
ex.ko: 클라이언트가 서버로 요청을 보낸다.

climate change.en: a long-term change in the world's weather patterns, especially due to human activity

ex.en: Climate change is causing more extreme weather events.
ex.ko: 기후 변화로 인해 극단적인 기상 현상이 더 많이 발생하고 있다.

clog.en: become blocked, or cause something to become blocked

ex.en: The drain is clogged with hair.
ex.ko: 배수구가 머리카락으로 막혔다.

clog.en: a shoe with a thick wooden sole

ex.en: She wore traditional wooden clogs at the festival.
ex.ko: 그녀는 축제에서 전통 나막신을 신었다.

closure.en: the act of shutting down a business, road, or facility permanently or temporarily

ex.en: The factory closure left hundreds of workers unemployed.
ex.ko: 그 공장의 폐쇄로 수백 명의 근로자가 실직했다.

closure.en: a feeling that an emotional experience or difficult period has been resolved

ex.en: She needed closure after the breakup.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이별 후 마음의 종결이 필요했다.

clothing.en: things that people wear to cover their bodies

ex.en: Winter clothing is essential in this climate.
ex.ko: 이런 기후에서는 겨울옷이 필수적이다.

clumsy.en: moving or doing things in an awkward way, often knocking things over

ex.en: He's so clumsy that he dropped the plate again.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 서툴러서 또 접시를 떨어뜨렸다.

clumsy.en: difficult to handle or use because of its size or shape

ex.en: The old machine is heavy and clumsy to move.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 기계는 무겁고 옮기기가 불편하다.

coaching.en: the job or activity of training and guiding someone, especially in sports or skills

ex.en: He got some coaching to improve his tennis serve.
ex.ko: 그는 테니스 서브를 향상시키기 위해 코칭을 좀 받았다.

coalition.en: a group formed by different political parties or groups working together for a common goal

ex.en: The two parties formed a coalition government.
ex.ko: 그 두 정당은 연립 정부를 구성했다.

coarse.en: having a rough texture, not smooth or fine

ex.en: The rope was made of coarse fibers.
ex.ko: 그 밧줄은 거친 섬유로 만들어졌다.

coarse.en: rude or lacking in refinement, especially in language or manners

ex.en: He made a coarse joke that offended everyone.
ex.ko: 그는 모두를 불쾌하게 만드는 상스러운 농담을 했다.

coastline.en: the outline or shape of a coast where land meets the sea

ex.en: The coastline stretches for hundreds of miles.
ex.ko: 그 해안선은 수백 마일에 걸쳐 뻗어 있다.

cocktail.en: a mixed alcoholic drink made from two or more ingredients

ex.en: She ordered a cocktail at the bar.
ex.ko: 그녀는 바에서 칵테일을 주문했다.

cocktail.en: a small dish of food served as an appetizer before a meal

ex.en: We started with a shrimp cocktail.
ex.ko: 우리는 새우 칵테일로 식사를 시작했다.

cocoa.en: a warm drink made from cocoa powder, milk, and sugar

ex.en: We drank hot cocoa by the fire.
ex.ko: 우리는 벽난로 옆에서 따뜻한 코코아를 마셨다.

cocoa.en: a brown powder made from roasted cacao beans, used in baking and drinks

ex.en: Add two tablespoons of cocoa to the batter.
ex.ko: 반죽에 코코아 가루 두 큰술을 넣으세요.

coconut.en: a large hard-shelled fruit with white flesh and liquid inside

ex.en: He cracked open the coconut to drink its water.
ex.ko: 그는 코코넛을 깨서 그 물을 마셨다.

coconut.en: a tall palm tree that produces coconuts

ex.en: Coconut trees line the beach.
ex.ko: 해변에 코코넛 야자나무들이 줄지어 있다.

coherence.en: the quality of ideas or parts fitting together in a logical and clear way

ex.en: The essay lacked coherence between paragraphs.
ex.ko: 그 에세이는 단락 사이의 일관성이 부족했다.

coherent.en: clear, logical, and well organized so it is easy to understand

ex.en: He gave a coherent explanation of the problem.
ex.ko: 그는 그 문제에 대해 조리 있는 설명을 했다.

coherent.en: able to think and speak clearly

ex.en: After the accident, he was barely coherent.
ex.ko: 사고 후 그는 거의 의식이 또렷하지 않았다.

coincide.en: happen at the same time as something else

ex.en: Her birthday coincides with the national holiday this year.
ex.ko: 올해 그녀의 생일이 국경일과 겹친다.

coincide.en: be in agreement or exactly the same as something else

ex.en: Our opinions on the matter coincide.
ex.ko: 그 문제에 대한 우리의 의견이 일치한다.

coincidence.en: a surprising situation in which two similar or related events happen at the same time by chance

ex.en: It was a coincidence that we were on the same flight.
ex.ko: 우리가 같은 비행기를 탄 것은 우연의 일치였다.

colleague.en: a person you work with, especially in a professional job

ex.en: She discussed the project with her colleagues.
ex.ko: 그녀는 동료들과 그 프로젝트에 대해 논의했다.

collector.en: a person who collects things such as art, coins, or stamps as a hobby

ex.en: He is a passionate stamp collector.
ex.ko: 그는 열정적인 우표 수집가이다.

collector.en: a person whose job is to collect payments such as taxes or bills

ex.en: The rent collector visits every month.
ex.ko: 집세 수금원이 매달 방문한다.

collocation.en: a combination of words that are often used together, such as 'make a decision'

ex.en: 'Heavy rain' is a common English collocation.
ex.ko: 'heavy rain'은 흔히 쓰이는 영어 연어이다.

colon.en: a punctuation mark (:) used before a list, example, or explanation

ex.en: Use a colon before listing examples.
ex.ko: 예시를 나열하기 전에는 콜론을 사용하세요.

colon.en: the main part of the large intestine in the body

ex.en: The doctor recommended a colon screening test.
ex.ko: 의사는 대장 검진을 권했다.

colonial.en: relating to a colony or the period when a country controlled another country as a colony

ex.en: The museum has exhibits from the colonial era.
ex.ko: 그 박물관에는 식민지 시대의 전시물이 있다.

coma.en: a state of deep unconsciousness from which a person cannot be woken

ex.en: He has been in a coma since the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 사고 이후 계속 혼수상태에 있다.

comfortably.en: in a way that feels relaxed and free from pain or worry

ex.en: She sat comfortably in the armchair.
ex.ko: 그녀는 안락의자에 편안하게 앉았다.

comfortably.en: with enough money to live without financial worry

ex.en: They live comfortably on his salary.
ex.ko: 그들은 그의 월급으로 경제적으로 여유롭게 산다.

commander.en: an officer who is in charge of a military unit or operation

ex.en: The commander gave orders to the troops.
ex.ko: 지휘관은 부대에 명령을 내렸다.

commemorate.en: remember and honor an important event or person, often with a ceremony

ex.en: The statue was built to commemorate the soldiers who died.
ex.ko: 그 동상은 전사한 군인들을 기념하기 위해 세워졌다.

commerce.en: the activity of buying and selling goods and services, especially on a large scale

ex.en: The internet has transformed global commerce.
ex.ko: 인터넷은 세계 상업을 변화시켰다.

commission.en: a fee paid to someone based on a percentage of sales or money they help bring in

ex.en: She earns a 10% commission on every sale.
ex.ko: 그녀는 판매마다 10%의 수수료를 받는다.

commission.en: formally ask someone to produce a piece of work for you

ex.en: The city commissioned an artist to paint a mural.
ex.ko: 시는 한 화가에게 벽화를 의뢰했다.

commission.en: an official group of people given a particular task or responsibility

ex.en: The commission investigated the cause of the accident.
ex.ko: 그 위원회는 사고 원인을 조사했다.

commonwealth.en: a nation or state, especially one organized for the common good of its people

ex.en: The constitution defines the rights of citizens in the commonwealth.
ex.ko: 헌법은 그 국가 공동체 시민의 권리를 규정한다.

commonwealth.en: an association of countries, especially former British colonies, that cooperate with each other

ex.en: Australia is a member of the Commonwealth.
ex.ko: 호주는 영연방의 회원국이다.

communicative.en: relating to the exchange of information or communication

ex.en: The course focuses on communicative skills in English.
ex.ko: 그 강좌는 영어 의사소통 능력에 중점을 둔다.

communicative.en: willing and able to talk to and share information with others

ex.en: She is very communicative and easy to talk to.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 대화하기 좋아하고 이야기 나누기 편하다.

community.en: the people who live in a particular area

ex.en: The whole community came together to clean the park.
ex.ko: 지역사회 전체가 공원을 청소하기 위해 함께 모였다.

community.en: a group of people who share the same interests, background, or identity

ex.en: She found support in the online gaming community.
ex.ko: 그녀는 온라인 게임 커뮤니티에서 지지를 얻었다.

commute.en: travel regularly between your home and your place of work

ex.en: He commutes to the city by train every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 기차로 도시까지 통근한다.

commute.en: the regular journey you make between home and work

ex.en: My commute takes about 40 minutes.
ex.ko: 내 통근 시간은 약 40분 걸린다.

commute.en: officially reduce a punishment to a less severe one

ex.en: The governor commuted his death sentence to life in prison.
ex.ko: 주지사는 그의 사형을 종신형으로 감형했다.

compassion.cue: 고통받는 사람에 대한 깊은 이해와 안타까움
compassion.en: a strong feeling of sympathy for someone who is suffering, and a desire to help them

ex.en: She showed great compassion for the injured animal.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다친 동물에게 큰 연민을 보였다.

compassionate.en: feeling or showing sympathy and concern for people who are suffering

ex.en: The nurse was very compassionate toward her patients.
ex.ko: 그 간호사는 환자들에게 매우 동정심이 많았다.

compatible.en: able to be used together with another device or system without problems

ex.en: This charger is compatible with most phone models.
ex.ko: 이 충전기는 대부분의 휴대폰 모델과 호환된다.

compatible.en: able to exist or work together well with something or someone else

ex.en: Their personalities are surprisingly compatible.
ex.ko: 그들의 성격은 놀랍도록 잘 맞는다.

compensate.en: pay someone money because they have suffered a loss or injury

ex.en: The company compensated the workers for their injuries.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 부상당한 근로자들에게 보상했다.

compensate.en: make up for a weakness or lack of something with something else

ex.en: He compensates for his lack of experience with hard work.
ex.ko: 그는 경험 부족을 노력으로 만회한다.

compensation.en: money paid to someone because they have suffered a loss, injury, or damage

ex.en: He received compensation for his workplace injury.
ex.ko: 그는 업무상 부상에 대해 보상금을 받았다.

competence.cue: 어떤 일을 잘 해낼 수 있는 능력
competence.en: the ability to do something well or to a required standard

ex.en: Her competence in project management impressed the board.
ex.ko: 그녀의 프로젝트 관리 역량은 이사회에 깊은 인상을 남겼다.

competent.en: having the skill or ability to do something well

ex.en: She is a highly competent manager.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 유능한 관리자이다.

compile.en: collect information from different sources and put it together in a report, book, or list

ex.en: She compiled a list of useful websites for students.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학생들을 위한 유용한 웹사이트 목록을 편집했다.

compile.en: use a computer program to translate code into a form the computer can run

ex.en: The program failed to compile due to a syntax error.
ex.ko: 그 프로그램은 구문 오류 때문에 컴파일에 실패했다.

completion.en: the act of finishing something completely

ex.en: The project is nearing completion.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 완료에 가까워지고 있다.

complexion.en: the natural color and appearance of the skin on someone's face

ex.en: She has a clear, glowing complexion.
ex.ko: 그녀는 맑고 빛나는 피부를 가지고 있다.

complexity.en: the state of being complicated or having many interconnected parts

ex.en: The complexity of the tax system confuses many people.
ex.ko: 세금 제도의 복잡성은 많은 사람들을 혼란스럽게 한다.

component.en: one of the parts that together make up a whole system or machine

ex.en: Each component of the engine must work correctly.
ex.ko: 엔진의 각 구성 부품은 제대로 작동해야 한다.

comprehension.en: the ability to understand the meaning of something, such as a text or speech

ex.en: The exercise is designed to improve reading comprehension.
ex.ko: 그 연습 문제는 독해력을 향상시키기 위해 고안되었다.

comprehensive.en: including everything that is necessary or relevant

ex.en: The report gives a comprehensive overview of the market.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 시장에 대한 포괄적인 개요를 제공한다.

compress.en: a cloth pad pressed against part of the body to reduce pain or swelling

ex.en: Apply a cold compress to reduce the swelling.
ex.ko: 부기를 줄이려면 찬 찜질팩을 대세요.

compress.en: press or reduce something so that it becomes smaller or takes up less space

ex.en: You can compress the files into a single folder.
ex.ko: 파일들을 하나의 폴더로 압축할 수 있다.

compression.en: the process of reducing the size of a file or data

ex.en: Image compression reduces file size without much quality loss.
ex.ko: 이미지 압축은 화질을 크게 떨어뜨리지 않고 파일 크기를 줄인다.

compression.en: the act of pressing something to make it smaller or denser

ex.en: The compression of the gas increased the pressure in the tank.
ex.ko: 가스의 압축은 탱크 내 압력을 증가시켰다.

compulsory.en: required by law or rule; not optional

ex.en: Wearing a seatbelt is compulsory in most countries.
ex.ko: 대부분의 나라에서 안전벨트 착용은 의무이다.

compute.en: calculate a number or amount using mathematics

ex.en: The software computes the total cost automatically.
ex.ko: 그 소프트웨어는 총비용을 자동으로 계산한다.

conceal.en: prevent something from being seen or known about

ex.en: She tried to conceal her disappointment.
ex.ko: 그녀는 실망감을 숨기려 애썼다.

concede.en: admit that something is true, often unwillingly

ex.en: He conceded that her argument made sense.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 주장이 일리 있다고 인정했다.

concede.en: admit that you have lost a competition or election

ex.en: The candidate conceded defeat shortly after midnight.
ex.ko: 그 후보는 자정 직후 패배를 인정했다.

conceit.en: excessive pride in yourself or your abilities

ex.en: His conceit made him unpopular with his colleagues.
ex.ko: 그의 자만심 때문에 그는 동료들 사이에서 인기가 없었다.

ex.en: It's conceivable that the plan could fail.
ex.ko: 그 계획이 실패할 수도 있다는 것은 있을 법한 일이다.

conceive.en: form an idea or plan in your mind

ex.en: She conceived the idea for the app while traveling.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행 중에 그 앱에 대한 아이디어를 생각해냈다.

conceive.en: imagine or understand something, especially as being possible

ex.en: I can't conceive of living without my phone.
ex.ko: 나는 휴대폰 없이 사는 것을 상상할 수가 없다.

ex.en: They had been trying to conceive for two years.
ex.ko: 그들은 2년 동안 임신을 시도해 왔다.

conception.en: an idea or a general understanding of something

ex.en: His conception of success is different from mine.
ex.ko: 성공에 대한 그의 개념은 나와 다르다.

conception.en: the moment when a woman becomes pregnant

ex.en: Conception usually occurs about two weeks after the start of a menstrual cycle.
ex.ko: 수태는 보통 생리 주기가 시작된 지 약 2주 후에 일어난다.

concerning.en: about or relating to a particular subject

ex.en: I have a question concerning the new policy.
ex.ko: 새 정책에 관해 질문이 있습니다.

concession.en: something you agree to give or allow in order to end a disagreement

ex.en: The union made a concession on working hours.
ex.ko: 노조는 근무 시간에 대해 양보했다.

concession.en: a stand or small shop selling food and drinks, especially at a stadium or theater

ex.en: We bought popcorn at the concession stand.
ex.ko: 우리는 매점에서 팝콘을 샀다.

concrete.en: a hard building material made from sand, cement, gravel, and water

ex.en: The sidewalk is made of concrete.
ex.ko: 그 보도는 콘크리트로 만들어졌다.

concrete.en: specific and definite, not vague or abstract

ex.en: We need concrete evidence before making an accusation.
ex.ko: 고발하기 전에 구체적인 증거가 필요하다.

condemn.en: express strong disapproval of something, usually because it is morally wrong

ex.en: The government condemned the attack.
ex.ko: 정부는 그 공격을 규탄했다.

condemn.en: officially state that a building is not safe to use

ex.en: The old house was condemned after the fire.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 집은 화재 이후 사용 금지 판정을 받았다.

conductor.en: a person who directs a group of musicians, especially an orchestra

ex.en: The conductor raised his baton to begin the symphony.
ex.ko: 지휘자가 교향곡을 시작하기 위해 지휘봉을 들었다.

conductor.en: a person whose job is to collect fares or tickets on a train or bus

ex.en: The conductor checked everyone's tickets.
ex.ko: 차장은 모든 사람의 표를 확인했다.

conductor.en: a substance that allows electricity or heat to pass through it easily

ex.en: Copper is an excellent conductor of electricity.
ex.ko: 구리는 전기가 잘 통하는 훌륭한 전도체이다.

cone.en: a cone-shaped object, such as the crispy container used for ice cream, or a basic geometric shape

ex.en: She ordered a chocolate ice cream cone.
ex.ko: 그녀는 초콜릿 아이스크림 콘을 주문했다.

cone.cue: 소나무 등에 열리는 방울 모양 열매
cone.en: the hard, scaly fruit of a pine or fir tree

ex.en: Pine cones fell from the tree in the wind.
ex.ko: 바람에 소나무에서 솔방울이 떨어졌다.

conference.cue: 많은 사람이 모여 논의하는 공식 회의
conference.en: a large formal meeting where people discuss a particular topic, often over several days

ex.en: She presented her research at an international conference.
ex.ko: 그녀는 국제 학회에서 자신의 연구를 발표했다.

conferencing.cue: 화면으로 서로 얼굴을 보며 하는 회의
conferencing.en: the activity of holding a meeting with people in different places using video and audio technology

ex.en: The team uses video conferencing to meet with clients abroad.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 해외 고객과의 만남에 화상회의를 이용한다.

confess.en: admit that you have done something wrong or committed a crime

ex.en: He confessed to stealing the money.
ex.ko: 그는 돈을 훔쳤다고 자백했다.

confess.en: tell a priest about your sins as part of a religious practice

ex.en: She went to confess her sins at church.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교회에서 자신의 죄를 고해하러 갔다.

confession.en: a statement admitting that you have done something wrong or committed a crime

ex.en: The suspect signed a written confession.
ex.ko: 용의자는 서면 자백서에 서명했다.

confession.en: (in the Catholic Church) the act of telling a priest your sins

ex.en: He went to confession every Sunday.
ex.ko: 그는 매주 일요일 고해성사를 하러 갔다.

confidently.en: in a way that shows belief in your own abilities

ex.en: She confidently answered every question.
ex.ko: 그녀는 모든 질문에 자신 있게 답했다.

ex.en: Please confine your answer to 100 words.
ex.ko: 답변을 100단어 이내로 제한해 주세요.

confine.en: keep someone in a place and prevent them from leaving

ex.en: The prisoner was confined to a small cell.
ex.ko: 그 죄수는 작은 감방에 갇혀 있었다.

confront.en: deal with a difficult situation or problem directly

ex.en: We must confront the challenges ahead.
ex.ko: 우리는 앞으로의 어려움에 맞서야 한다.

confront.en: approach someone directly to challenge or accuse them of something

ex.en: She confronted him about the missing money.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사라진 돈에 대해 그에게 따졌다.

conjunction.en: a word such as 'and', 'but', or 'because' that connects words, phrases, or clauses

ex.en: 'And' and 'but' are common conjunctions.
ex.ko: 'and'와 'but'은 흔한 접속사이다.

conjunction.en: the state of being joined together or happening together

ex.en: The success came from a conjunction of hard work and luck.
ex.ko: 그 성공은 노력과 운의 결합에서 비롯되었다.

conquest.en: the act of taking control of a country, place, or people by force

ex.en: The Roman conquest of Britain began in 43 AD.
ex.ko: 로마의 브리튼 정복은 서기 43년에 시작되었다.

conscience.en: the inner sense that tells you whether your actions are right or wrong

ex.en: His conscience wouldn't let him lie.
ex.ko: 그의 양심이 거짓말을 하도록 놔두지 않았다.

consciousness.en: the state of being awake and aware of your surroundings

ex.en: She lost consciousness after the fall.
ex.ko: 그녀는 넘어진 후 의식을 잃었다.

consciousness.en: awareness of a particular issue or problem, especially in society

ex.en: The campaign aims to raise consciousness about climate change.
ex.ko: 그 캠페인은 기후 변화에 대한 인식을 높이는 것을 목표로 한다.

consensus.en: a general agreement reached by a group of people

ex.en: The committee reached a consensus on the new policy.
ex.ko: 위원회는 새 정책에 대해 합의에 도달했다.

consent.en: permission or agreement to do something

ex.en: The doctor needs your consent before the surgery.
ex.ko: 의사는 수술 전에 당신의 동의가 필요하다.

consent.en: agree to do or allow something

ex.en: She consented to the interview after some hesitation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 약간 망설인 끝에 인터뷰에 동의했다.

considerably.en: to a large degree; quite a lot

ex.en: Prices have risen considerably this year.
ex.ko: 올해 물가가 상당히 올랐다.

considering.en: taking into account a particular fact

ex.en: Considering the weather, we should leave early.
ex.ko: 날씨를 고려하면 우리는 일찍 출발해야 한다.

consistent.en: always behaving or happening in the same way

ex.en: He has been consistent in his performance all season.
ex.ko: 그는 시즌 내내 일관된 실력을 보여주었다.

consistent.en: in agreement with something else; not contradicting it

ex.en: Her story is consistent with what the witness said.
ex.ko: 그녀의 이야기는 목격자가 말한 것과 일치한다.

consolation.en: something that makes you feel better after a disappointment or sadness

ex.en: It was some consolation that he tried his best.
ex.ko: 그가 최선을 다했다는 것이 어느 정도 위안이 되었다.

console.en: make someone who is sad or disappointed feel better

ex.en: She tried to console her friend after the loss.
ex.ko: 그녀는 상실을 겪은 친구를 위로하려 했다.

console.en: a device connected to a screen that is used to play video games

ex.en: He bought the latest gaming console.
ex.ko: 그는 최신 게임 콘솔을 샀다.

constant.en: happening all the time or repeatedly, without stopping

ex.en: The constant noise from traffic kept her awake.
ex.ko: 끊임없는 교통 소음 때문에 그녀는 잠을 못 잤다.

constant.en: a value or number that does not change

ex.en: Pi is a mathematical constant.
ex.ko: 파이는 수학 상수이다.

consult.en: go to a person or source for advice or information

ex.en: You should consult a doctor about that pain.
ex.ko: 그 통증에 대해 의사와 상담해야 한다.

consult.en: look at a book, map, or other source to find information

ex.en: Consult the manual if you have any questions.
ex.ko: 궁금한 점이 있으면 설명서를 참고하세요.

consultant.en: a person who gives expert advice professionally

ex.en: They hired a marketing consultant to boost sales.
ex.ko: 그들은 매출을 늘리기 위해 마케팅 컨설턴트를 고용했다.

contaminate.en: make something dirty, polluted, or unsafe by adding a harmful substance

ex.en: Chemicals from the factory contaminated the river.
ex.ko: 공장에서 나온 화학물질이 강을 오염시켰다.

contemporary.en: belonging to or happening in the present time

ex.en: The museum features contemporary art.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 현대 미술을 전시한다.

contemporary.en: a person who lived or worked at the same time as someone else

ex.en: Mozart and Haydn were contemporaries.
ex.ko: 모차르트와 하이든은 동시대인이었다.

contempt.en: a strong feeling that someone or something deserves no respect

ex.en: She looked at him with contempt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그를 경멸하는 눈빛으로 바라보았다.

contempt.en: the crime of refusing to obey or respect a court's authority

ex.en: He was held in contempt of court for refusing to testify.
ex.ko: 그는 증언을 거부해 법정 모독으로 처벌받았다.

contract.en: a written or spoken agreement that is legally binding

ex.en: They signed a two-year contract.
ex.ko: 그들은 2년 계약서에 서명했다.

contract.en: make a formal agreement to do work or provide a service

ex.en: The city contracted a firm to build the bridge.
ex.ko: 시는 다리 건설을 위해 한 회사와 계약을 맺었다.

ex.en: He contracted malaria while traveling abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 해외여행 중 말라리아에 걸렸다.

ex.en: Muscles contract when you exercise.
ex.ko: 운동을 하면 근육이 수축한다.

contradict.en: be in conflict with, or say the opposite of, another statement or fact

ex.en: His story contradicts what the witness said.
ex.ko: 그의 이야기는 목격자가 한 말과 모순된다.

contradict.en: say that something someone else has said is not true

ex.en: She contradicted her boss during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중 상사의 말을 반박했다.

contradiction.cue: 두 가지가 서로 맞지 않는 상태
contradiction.en: a situation in which two facts, ideas, or statements are opposed to each other

ex.en: There's a contradiction between what he said and what he did.
ex.ko: 그가 한 말과 행동 사이에는 모순이 있다.

contradictory.en: saying or being the opposite of something else, so that both cannot be true

ex.en: The two reports gave contradictory accounts of the accident.
ex.ko: 그 두 보고서는 사고에 대해 서로 모순되는 설명을 내놓았다.

controversy.cue: 사람들 사이에 의견이 크게 갈리는 논쟁
controversy.en: a lot of public disagreement and argument about something

ex.en: The new policy caused a lot of controversy.
ex.ko: 그 새 정책은 많은 논란을 일으켰다.

conveniently.en: in a way that is easy or suitable for a particular purpose

ex.en: The hotel is conveniently located near the airport.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 공항 근처에 편리하게 위치해 있다.

convention.en: a traditional way of doing something that most people in a society follow

ex.en: It's a social convention to shake hands when meeting someone.
ex.ko: 누군가를 만날 때 악수하는 것은 사회적 관습이다.

convention.en: a large formal meeting of members of a profession, group, or political party

ex.en: Thousands attended the annual convention.
ex.ko: 수천 명이 연례 총회에 참석했다.

conventional.en: following what is traditional or generally accepted, rather than new or different

ex.en: She prefers conventional methods of teaching.
ex.ko: 그녀는 전통적인 교수법을 선호한다.

conventional.en: (of weapons) using explosives rather than nuclear power

ex.en: The war was fought with conventional weapons.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁은 재래식 무기로 치러졌다.

ex.en: They conversed quietly in the corner.
ex.ko: 그들은 구석에서 조용히 대화를 나눴다.

converse.en: the opposite or reverse of a statement or situation

ex.en: The converse of this argument is also true.
ex.ko: 이 주장의 정반대 명제 또한 참이다.

conversely.en: in a way that is the opposite of what has just been said

ex.en: Conversely, low prices can also indicate poor quality.
ex.ko: 반대로 낮은 가격이 품질이 나쁘다는 것을 나타낼 수도 있다.

conversion.en: the process of changing something from one form, use, or unit to another

ex.en: The conversion from Celsius to Fahrenheit requires a formula.
ex.ko: 섭씨에서 화씨로의 변환에는 공식이 필요하다.

conversion.en: the act of changing your religion or beliefs

ex.en: His conversion to Buddhism surprised his family.
ex.ko: 그가 불교로 개종한 것은 가족을 놀라게 했다.

convert.en: change something from one form, system, or purpose to another

ex.en: They converted the old factory into apartments.
ex.ko: 그들은 낡은 공장을 아파트로 전환했다.

convert.en: change your religion, or persuade someone else to change theirs

ex.en: He converted to Islam after moving abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 해외로 이주한 후 이슬람교로 개종했다.

convinced.en: completely sure that something is true

ex.en: I'm convinced that she is telling the truth.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 진실을 말하고 있다고 확신한다.

cooperate.en: work together with someone else to achieve a common goal

ex.en: The two companies agreed to cooperate on the project.
ex.ko: 그 두 회사는 그 프로젝트에서 협력하기로 합의했다.

cooperation.en: the act of working together with someone to achieve a shared goal

ex.en: The project succeeded thanks to close cooperation between teams.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 팀 간의 긴밀한 협력 덕분에 성공했다.

cooperative.en: willing to work together with others and be helpful

ex.en: The staff were very cooperative during the inspection.
ex.ko: 직원들은 점검 중에 매우 협조적이었다.

cooperative.en: a business or organization owned and run jointly by its members

ex.en: The farmers formed a cooperative to sell their produce.
ex.ko: 그 농부들은 농산물을 판매하기 위해 협동조합을 결성했다.

cope.cue: 힘든 상황을 잘 견디며 헤쳐 나가다
cope.en: deal successfully with a difficult situation

ex.en: She is learning to cope with stress at work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직장에서의 스트레스에 대처하는 법을 배우고 있다.

copper.en: a reddish-brown metal used in wires and pipes

ex.en: The pipes in this house are made of copper.
ex.ko: 이 집의 배관은 구리로 만들어져 있다.

ex.en: The coppers arrived just in time.
ex.ko: 경찰관들이 때마침 도착했다.

coral.en: a hard, colorful substance formed in the sea by the skeletons of small marine animals

ex.en: Divers explored the coral reef.
ex.ko: 다이버들은 산호초를 탐험했다.

coral.en: having a strong pink or pinkish-orange color

ex.en: She wore a coral dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 산호색 드레스를 입고 갔다.

core.en: the central or innermost part of something

ex.en: The Earth's core is extremely hot.
ex.ko: 지구의 핵은 매우 뜨겁다.

core.en: the most important or essential part of an idea, problem, or plan

ex.en: Trust is at the core of any good relationship.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 좋은 관계의 핵심이다.

cornerstone.en: the basic and most important part on which something is built or developed

ex.en: Trust is the cornerstone of a good marriage.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 좋은 결혼 생활의 초석이다.

cornerstone.en: a stone laid at the corner of a building, often marking its foundation

ex.en: The mayor laid the cornerstone of the new city hall.
ex.ko: 시장은 새 시청의 초석을 놓았다.

cornet.en: a brass musical instrument similar to a trumpet, with a bright sound

ex.en: He played the cornet in the school band.
ex.ko: 그는 학교 밴드에서 코넷을 연주했다.

corona.en: the outer layer of the sun's atmosphere, visible as a glowing ring during a solar eclipse

ex.en: You can see the sun's corona during a total eclipse.
ex.ko: 개기일식 때 태양의 코로나를 볼 수 있다.

corporal.en: relating to the body rather than the mind

ex.en: Corporal punishment is banned in many schools.
ex.ko: 많은 학교에서 체벌은 금지되어 있다.

corporal.en: a rank in the army just above private

ex.en: He was promoted to corporal after two years of service.
ex.ko: 그는 2년 복무 후 상병으로 진급했다.

corporate.en: relating to a large company or corporation

ex.en: She works in corporate finance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기업 금융 분야에서 일한다.

corporation.en: a large company or group of companies authorized to act as a single legal entity

ex.en: He works for a multinational corporation.
ex.ko: 그는 다국적 기업에서 일한다.

correctness.en: the quality of being right or accurate

ex.en: Please check the correctness of your answers.
ex.ko: 답의 정확성을 확인해 주세요.

correctness.en: the quality of following accepted social standards, especially about not offending groups of people

ex.en: The debate over political correctness continues in the media.
ex.ko: 정치적 올바름에 대한 논쟁이 언론에서 계속되고 있다.

correspond.en: match or be similar to something else

ex.en: Her account of events corresponds with his.
ex.ko: 그녀의 사건 설명은 그의 것과 일치한다.

correspond.en: communicate with someone regularly by writing letters or emails

ex.en: They have been corresponding for years.
ex.ko: 그들은 수년간 서신을 주고받아 왔다.

correspondence.en: letters or emails exchanged between people

ex.en: All correspondence should be sent to this address.
ex.ko: 모든 서신은 이 주소로 보내야 한다.

correspondence.en: a close similarity or agreement between two things

ex.en: There is a close correspondence between the two theories.
ex.ko: 그 두 이론 사이에는 밀접한 일치가 있다.

correspondent.en: a journalist who reports news, especially from a foreign country

ex.en: She works as a foreign correspondent for a major newspaper.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한 대형 신문사의 해외 특파원으로 일한다.

corresponding.en: matching or similar to something else, often in position or function

ex.en: Each number has a corresponding letter.
ex.ko: 각 숫자에는 해당하는 알파벳이 있다.

corridor.en: a long passage inside a building with rooms on one or both sides

ex.en: The classrooms are along this corridor.
ex.ko: 교실들은 이 복도를 따라 있다.

corrupt.en: willing to act dishonestly for money or power, especially if in a position of authority

ex.en: The corrupt official accepted bribes for years.
ex.ko: 그 부패한 공무원은 수년간 뇌물을 받았다.

corrupt.en: (of a file or data) containing errors and unable to be used properly

ex.en: The download failed and the file was corrupt.
ex.ko: 다운로드가 실패해서 그 파일이 손상되었다.

corrupt.en: cause someone to become dishonest or morally bad

ex.en: Power can corrupt even good people.
ex.ko: 권력은 선한 사람조차 타락시킬 수 있다.
